mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and discount future equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and triggers recession pricing, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent over a 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds sharply following a macroeconomic shock, signaling restored manufacturing capacity and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of >0.5 percent MoM during recovery phases yield negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL exceeding 3.5 percent forces markets to price in prolonged monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5 percent, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue bases, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS MoM changes correlate with negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with elevated inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force aggressive rate hikes, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Quarters with top-quartile GDPC1 growth and CPIAUCSL above 3.0 percent show positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent indicates an acute supply chain disruption or demand collapse, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 1.0 percent MoM are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in >65% of cases.
